texto: (...) I think your definition changes based on your experiences." (age twenty-two, bisexual)Six years later, this same woman noted:"I date both men and women, but i don't like the word "bisexual", because I think it implies polarity. I guess I started thinking about this around 4 1/2 years ago, when I was involved in a long-term committed relationship with a man, but a queer man. And it made me redefine things, because I didn't believe that a queer man and a queer woman together in a relationship like ours was conventionally heterosexual." (age twenty-eight, bisexual)
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Lisa Diamond: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “(...) I think your definition changes based on your experiences." (age twenty-two, bisexual)Six y…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, queer, sexuality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e sexuality.

texto: For those of us who question, your whole life becomes a question. Do you then reach some level of understanding, and then it's static? I don't think so" (age twenty-two, unlabeled)
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Lisa Diamond que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “For those of us who question, your whole life becomes a question. Do you then reach some level of…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, politics, sexuality, com foco em sexuality e women.
